hints.anni: 'anni' is a noun — the subject of the sentence

steps.target: It is a deponent verb — passive in form, active in meaning
choices: It is a deponent verb — passive in form, active in meaning
It is in the passive voice

hints.It is in the passive voice: 'labuntur' looks passive but is deponent — the meaning is active
hints.It is a defective verb: Defective verbs lack forms — 'labi' has all forms but is deponent
hints.It is subjunctive: Subjunctive would be 'labantur' — 'labuntur' is present indicative

hints.laboro, laborare, laboravi, laboratum: These are the principal parts of 'laborare' (to work)
hints.lavo, lavare, lavi, lautum: These are the principal parts of 'lavare' (to wash)
hints.laudo, laudare, laudavi, laudatum: These are the principal parts of 'laudare' (to praise)

hints.Genitive: Genitive singular of 'annus' is also 'anni', but here it is nominative plural (subject of 'labuntur')
hints.Vocative: Vocative plural would also be 'anni', but the context shows these are the subject

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'annus' with -us ending is 2nd declension
hints.3rd: 3rd declension nouns have varied endings — 'annus' is a regular 2nd declension -us noun
hints.4th: 4th declension -us nouns have genitive -us — 'anni' genitive indicates 2nd declension

hints.Adverb: Adverbs modify verbs — 'Eheu' is an exclamation of grief, an interjection
hints.Conjunction: Conjunctions join clauses — 'Eheu' expresses emotion, making it an interjection
hints.Preposition: Prepositions govern nouns — 'Eheu' is a standalone exclamation

translation: Alas, the fleeting years slip away
